head: 5432c90
handoff_notice: HARD RULE (CLAUDE.md): Claude and Codex must never commit to the same branch. claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 remains Claude-owned. SECURITY: two credential-exposure incidents occurred earlier this session (see Critical Anti-Patterns below) -- both were remediated by rotation. ARCHITECTURE: a separate, unrelated false-blocker mistake (aws-admin-prod 'needs a real prod account') was also found and corrected this session -- see decisions.

completed_tasks.name: Parameterized mdm_create_instance.sql and mdm_create_network_policy.sql by environment via Snow CLI Jinja templating; updated go-live.sh's 'Snowflake Postgres / graph prerequisites' stage to compute env-specific names and invoke the network-policy script (previously missing from the pipeline).

Created the missing EDGARTOOLS_PROD.MDM schema (ad-hoc CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS, mirroring how dev's MDM schema was created -- no IaC tracks this in either environment) so mdm_create_network_policy.sql's USE SCHEMA could succeed.
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- live Snowflake DDL only

completed_tasks.name: Ran the parameterized SQL against the edgartools-prod Snowflake connection: created network rule + network policy (edgartools_prod_mdm_postgres_policy), then provisioned EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM Postgres instance (state READY).
completed_tasks.commit: live Snowflake DDL; evidence in f24cb99

completed_tasks.name: Fixed two credential-redaction gaps in go-live.sh's redact_text(): (1) added --format JSON to the mdm_create_instance.sql invocation and a pattern matching the snowflake_admin/application/admin_password JSON keys CREATE POSTGRES INSTANCE emits; (2) added the generic 'password' key (used by ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... RESET ACCESS FOR) after a second leak. Also made execute_stage() redact all stage output by default, and added missing --enable-templating JINJA flags.

Disproved a false decision recorded in a prior HANDOFF.json: Snowflake prod and dev are NOT separate accounts. SELECT CURRENT_ACCOUNT()/CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME()/CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() returned identical values (NP05015 / YW12138 / IXKYQWK) via both the edgartools-prod and snowconn (dev) connections. EDGARTOOLS_DEV and EDGARTOOLS_PROD are two databases in one shared account -- matching the already-documented AWS same-account pattern. Corrected the decision entry and the evidence file.
Re-ran Plan 08-01 Task 1 (precondition check). Outcome: CONFIRMED -- EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM exists and is READY.

Rotated EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM's snowflake_admin and application credentials via ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... RESET ACCESS FOR, after the original CREATE POSTGRES INSTANCE output and a first rotation attempt both leaked credentials into chat (see Critical Anti-Patterns). Third rotation attempt, run after fixing redact_text, redacted cleanly -- current credentials were never printed.
completed_tasks.commit: 10be2d9 (script fix); rotation itself is live Snowflake state, not a git change

completed_tasks.name: Disproved a recurring false blocker repeated across STATE.md, 08-01-SUMMARY.md, the evidence file, and a prior HANDOFF.json: aws-admin-prod resolving to account 077127448006 was wrongly treated as 'the dev account, needs a real prod account.' D-05 (v1.5 Phase 2, .planning/workstreams/go-live/milestones/v1.5-phases/02-aws-and-snowflake-production-deployment-dry-run/02-CONTEXT.md) already settled this: aws-admin-dev and aws-admin-prod are INTENTIONALLY the same account; prod is a same-account, prefix-distinguished resource set, not a separate account. Verified live: edgartools-prod-* S3 buckets exist, and edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn + edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake secrets already exist (created by the prod Terraform apply, 2026-06-19) with VersionIdsToStages: null -- created but never populated. Corrected STATE.md Blocker 2 and the evidence file.

remaining_tasks.name: Plan 08-01 Task 2: populate edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn (via bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin) and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake (raw put-secret-value, 7 keys per the plan) using the rotated EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM application credential. NOT blocked -- both secrets exist, unpopulated, aws-admin-prod is correctly configured per D-05. Has not been executed yet pending explicit operator go-ahead to write real production secret values (a meaningful, hard-to-reverse action).

Plan 08-01 Task 3: edit the HANDOFF.json blocker description to clarify neo4j/api_keys are out of scope for Phase 8 (only postgres_dsn + snowflake are MDM-02). Pre-satisfied by a prior session per 08-01-SUMMARY.md -- verify and close out, no edit expected to be needed.
Plan 08-02: connectivity/migrate/counts verification + launch-gate-matrix checkpoint. Cannot start until 08-01 Task 2 completes (secrets must exist populated for connectivity checks).
Confirm with user whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password (exposed in chat in an earlier, unrelated session) has been rotated. Still unconfirmed.
Hosted graph E2E (Phase 9), dashboard UAT (Phase 10), final GO decision (Phase 11).

blockers.description: ANANP11 Snowflake password exposure from an earlier session remains unconfirmed-rotated. Unrelated to this session's incidents.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password has been rotated (separate, earlier-session incident).
Explicit go-ahead to write real production secret values for edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake (Task 2).
human_actions_pending.context: Both secrets exist and are unpopulated; nothing technical blocks this, but writing real production secrets is treated as requiring explicit confirmation, not assumed authorization.

decisions.decision: Snowflake prod and dev are the SAME account (NP05015 / YW12138 / IXKYQWK), distinguished only by database name (EDGARTOOLS_DEV vs EDGARTOOLS_PROD) -- not separate accounts as a prior session incorrectly concluded.
decisions.rationale: SELECT CURRENT_ACCOUNT(), CURRENT_ACCOUNT_NAME(), CURRENT_ORGANIZATION_NAME() returned identical values via both the edgartools-prod and snowconn (dev) connections. Matches the already-documented AWS same-account, prefix-distinguished pattern.

decisions.decision: Snowflake Postgres instances and network policies are account-scoped, not database-scoped -- so EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM and EDGARTOOLS_DEV_MDM (and their network policies) share one blast radius within the single shared account.
decisions.rationale: Direct consequence of the account-separation correction above; confirmed by SHOW POSTGRES INSTANCES listing both instances regardless of which connection (dev or prod) issued the query.

decisions.decision: snow sql's default --enable-templating mode (LEGACY) does NOT support {{ }} Jinja syntax used by the parameterized SQL files; --enable-templating JINJA must be passed explicitly.
decisions.rationale: Discovered empirically: running the network-policy SQL without the flag raised a syntax error on the literal '{{ schema }}' string.

decisions.decision: grep/sed-based credential redaction must be verified against the EXACT output shape of the command being filtered, not assumed from a generically-named pattern like 'password|secret|token'. Two separate redaction-gap incidents this session both stemmed from key names that did not match the existing filter pattern.
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md 5-whys discipline -- the root cause both times was an unverified assumption about output shape, not a one-off typo.

decisions.decision: aws-admin-prod resolving to account 077127448006 is CORRECT, by-design behavior -- not a blocker. Per D-05 (v1.5 Phase 2), aws-admin-dev and aws-admin-prod are intentionally the same AWS account; prod is a same-account, prefix-distinguished resource set (separate Terraform root, :prod-tagged images, edgartools-prod-* named resources), not a separate account. This reverses a false blocker repeated across STATE.md, 08-01-SUMMARY.md, the evidence file, and a prior HANDOFF.json, all of which wrongly called 077127448006 'the dev account' and demanded a 'genuine production AWS account.'
decisions.rationale: D-05 is an explicit, already-settled architecture decision recorded in 02-CONTEXT.md, repeated consistently across multiple v1.5 phase artifacts (01-LAUNCH-GATE-MATRIX.md, 02-RESEARCH.md, 02-DISCUSSION-LOG.md, 03-PATTERNS.md). Live verification confirms: edgartools-prod-* S3 buckets exist and are visible via aws-admin-prod; edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake secrets already exist (created by the prod Terraform apply on 2026-06-19) with VersionIdsToStages: null, i.e. created but never populated -- exactly Task 2's expected starting state.

uncommitted_files: infra/aws-prod-application.json (intentionally untracked -- generated deploy manifest, never to be committed per D-10)
next_action: Get explicit operator go-ahead, then run Plan 08-01 Task 2: populate edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn (via bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin) and edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake (via raw put-secret-value, 7 keys per the plan) using the freshly-rotated EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM application credential -- NOT the original or first-rotation values, both compromised. Then verify Task 3 is still satisfied, then Plan 08-02.
context_notes: This session closed BOTH gaps blocking Phase 8: the Snowflake side (instance provisioned, network policy created, credentials rotated after two redaction-gap incidents) and a separate false AWS blocker (aws-admin-prod resolving to 077127448006 was incorrectly treated as needing a distinct prod account, when D-05 already settled that this is the correct same-account design and both target secrets already exist, unpopulated). Task 2 is technically unblocked but was not executed in this session -- writing real production secret values is treated as requiring an explicit go-ahead, not assumed from 'unblock the path' framing. See Critical Anti-Patterns in .continue-here.md before running any further Postgres-instance SQL through go-live.sh or manually.
